recent_calls.summary: The user, Cherise, called after business hours requesting a furnace replacement. The agent informed her that a live representative was unavailable but documented her request and arranged for a callback after 4:30 PM the next business day. The call ended with the user confirming the callback time and expressing thanks.

The user called after hours and requested to speak to a live agent. The assistant explained that live agents are not available after hours and could not connect the user to one. The call ended shortly after without further resolution.
The user called after hours and requested to speak to a live person, but the agent informed them that live assistance was unavailable. The call ended abruptly shortly after.

The user called Budget's after-hours assistant to schedule a repair for their cooling system. The agent began gathering details about the issue before the call ended abruptly. The conversation was brief and did not reach a resolution.

Jason called to inquire about the status of his AC repair, which is pending a warranty part after a technician visit. The agent confirmed the details and assured Jason that the request would be passed along for follow-up on the next business day. Jason wants the repair done as soon as possible and provided no special instructions for access.

Derwin called about his AC unit which is running but not cooling the house. He requested service either today or next Tuesday at his address in Hazelwood, and confirmed his callback number. He also expressed interest in receiving information about home maintenance programs. The agent confirmed all details and assured that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing will contact him soon.

Karen called to report that her air conditioner appointment was missed and to inquire about the status and possible rescheduling. The agent informed her that since it was after-hours, no one was available to assist immediately but assured her that the team would follow up on the next business day. Karen declined further assistance and planned to call back in the morning.

The user called to get the results from their appointment. The agent informed the user that they do not have access to the results but will pass the request to the appropriate team for follow-up. The call ended with the user declining further assistance.

The user called Budget and spoke briefly with Sophie, indicating they had a home warranty service request. The user then requested to speak to the office manager, and the call was successfully transferred to another agent.
The user requested to be connected to the billing department, and the agent promptly transferred the call to the billing team. The transfer was successful without any issues.

The user called to check the status of a technician appointment scheduled for the same day. The agent confirmed the user's details and informed them that the technician was scheduled to arrive between 1 and 2 PM, but the user noted the technician had not arrived by 3:30 PM. The call was then transferred to a live agent for further assistance.
The user called Budget by mistake, intending to reach a plumber or installer, and apologized for the confusion before ending the call.

John called to schedule routine spring maintenance for his air conditioner. The agent provided available morning slots, and John selected Thursday, May 21 at 10 AM, which was successfully booked. John gave positive feedback about the quick scheduling process.
The user requested to be connected to a representative, and the agent successfully transferred the call to a live agent.
The user requested to be connected to the billing department, and the agent promptly transferred the call to the billing team. The transfer was successful without any issues.

The user, Haley, returned a missed call and spoke briefly with Sophie from Budget. Sophie acknowledged the callback and transferred Haley to a live agent for further assistance. The call ended with a successful transfer.
The user called and requested to speak with Amy. The agent attempted to connect the user and successfully transferred the call to another agent.
The user requested to speak to Amy, and the agent transferred the call to the appropriate live agent successfully. The call lasted 13 seconds and ended with a call transfer.
The user requested to speak with Amy, and the agent transferred the call to a live agent who could assist. The transfer was completed successfully.

The user called after receiving a text about scheduling a service related to their American Home Shield home warranty, specifically for cooling. The agent attempted to clarify the service needed but the user expressed frustration about repeating information. The call was then transferred to a live agent for further assistance.

The user called and provided their ZIP code, then requested to speak with a representative. The agent acknowledged the request and successfully transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

The user inquired about service stickers on a furnace they were buying and whether Budget was the current service provider. The agent explained the presence of multiple stickers and offered to connect the user with a customer service representative. The call was then successfully transferred to a live agent for further assistance.

The user requested that Nicole call them after 4:30 PM regarding a plumbing service request made the previous day. The agent confirmed details about the service and the user's role as the homeowner before noting the callback request.

Kennedy from American Home Shield called to follow up on a dispatch for Shi Huang at 4134 Westminster Place, requesting a status update on the work order. The agent was unable to find an appointment and offered to transfer Kennedy to a live representative, which was done successfully.
The user received a call and the agent attempted to connect them with a live agent for further assistance. The call was successfully transferred to another number.
The agent greeted the user and attempted to connect them with a live representative. The call was then successfully transferred to another agent for further assistance.

The user called to confirm an appointment for tomorrow and verified their contact number with the agent. The user then requested to speak with a live representative, and the agent successfully transferred the call to another agent.

The call involved Tammy from American Home Shield providing a dispatch number and details for a customer named Jack Land who needs a follow-up service for a malfunctioning air conditioning unit. The agent confirmed the service details, appointment preferences, and contact information, and the user declined information about maintenance programs. The call ended positively with the user providing excellent feedback.
The user called to return a phone call to Nicole, but Nicole was unavailable. The agent attempted to connect the user to a live agent who could assist further and successfully transferred the call.
The call was very brief, with the agent greeting the user and asking how to assist, but the user hung up immediately without responding.
The user called to pay $152 for an HVAC repair service. The agent informed the user that billing information was not accessible and transferred the call to the billing team for assistance.
The user called to get a quote for a new air conditioner. The agent attempted to connect the user with a live agent for further assistance and successfully transferred the call.

Keoni from American Home Shield called Budget regarding a dispatch number and a follow-up service that was supposed to occur but had not been completed. The agent acknowledged the issue and transferred the call to a customer service representative for further assistance.

The user called and asked if the agent was a real person. The agent clarified that she is a virtual assistant and offered help, but the user ended the call shortly after without requesting any services.
The call was very brief and ended abruptly after the user said "Sorry. Roll over." The agent was unable to assist the user due to the short duration of the call.

Andrea from American Home Shield called to schedule a home warranty service dispatch for Philip Berwick's air conditioning that is not working. The agent collected all necessary details, noted the request as flexible, and confirmed that Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing will contact Philip to arrange the appointment. The user provided feedback requesting a live agent option for scheduling.

The user, Emma Hutchinson, called to confirm the time frame for a technician appointment scheduled for the next day. The agent confirmed the appointment details with Emma, who was satisfied with the information and had no further questions. The call ended with the user expressing thanks and no additional requests.
The user requested to speak with a live agent, and the initial agent transferred the call to another live agent successfully. The call lasted 13 seconds and ended with a call transfer.
The user called Budget and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent, Sophie, greeted the user and initiated the transfer without further conversation.

The user called to schedule an air conditioner tune-up for the summer and successfully booked an appointment for Friday, May 22nd, from 10 AM to 12 PM at their home in Saint Louis. The agent confirmed the booking and requested feedback, which the user rated as average, expressing a preference to speak to a human. The call ended with the appointment confirmed and no further issues.

The user called to confirm their appointment scheduled for May 19th between 10 and 11 AM with technician Vernon. The agent verified the user's name and phone number, confirmed the appointment details, and the user had no further questions or updates. The call ended after confirming the appointment.
The user called to confirm an appointment and provided their full name. The agent attempted to verify the user's contact information but the call ended abruptly before completion.

The user informed the agent that someone is at home and confirmed an upcoming AC service appointment. The agent inquired about the status of the AC and the user's role related to the home, but the call ended shortly after without further details.

The user called the agent but quickly realized they had dialed the wrong number and apologized. The agent confirmed if the user needed assistance with a job number, but the user declined and ended the call.

The user called to inquire about an update on a technician scheduled to arrive within a specified time window. The agent attempted to assist but transferred the call to a live agent for further help. The call ended with a successful transfer.

The user, Sherry Wise, called to check on the timing of a service appointment. The agent confirmed the user's details but found no appointment scheduled for that day. The user requested to be transferred to a live representative, and the agent successfully transferred the call.

The user called to inquire about the estimated arrival time of the AC technician for a service appointment at 5533 Donbar Drive in Saint Louis. The agent confirmed the appointment details and informed the user that the technician, Devon, is scheduled to arrive between 11 AM and 12 PM. The call ended with the agent transferring the user to a live agent for further assistance.

The user, Ronald W. Barnes, called to check the status of an appointment scheduled for today with technician Brooks between 11 AM and 12 PM. The agent confirmed the appointment details and the user's contact information. The user did not request any changes or additional assistance.

The user, Tanisha Snipes, requested a plumbing service appointment for a water heater with a vapor sensor issue at 142 E Etta Ave, St. Louis, MO. The agent confirmed the details and successfully booked the appointment for 8 AM the next day. The call ended after the booking was confirmed and the agent requested feedback.
